senses.definition: to consider someone or something as important or deserving of attention

examples.x: She takes her health seriously by following a balanced diet and exercising regularly.
It's essential to take environmental concerns seriously and act to protect the planet.

senses.definition: to position oneself or others on the side of a group for protection or a strategic advantage, typically in a military context

examples.x: During the sports game, they took the flank positions to outmaneuver the opposing team.
In the military operation, the soldiers took the flank to provide cover for the main force.
The experienced hikers knew to take the flank while navigating through the challenging terrain.
The security team took the flank positions to secure the perimeter of the event.
In the battle, the skilled archers were instructed to take the flank, ensuring the army's defenses from the side.

senses.definition: to set aside a portion of time for a specific activity, task, or purpose

examples.x: She takes the time to read a book before going to bed.
He took the time to research and plan his upcoming trip.
In the morning, I will take the time to meditate for relaxation and mindfulness.
He takes the time to listen to his employees' concerns and ideas.
They take the time to bond with their children by playing games together.

senses.definition: to spend as much as time one needs on doing something without hurrying

examples.x: Don't worry about the deadline; you can take your time to finish the project.
She always takes her time when cooking a special meal for her family.
In the art gallery, visitors can take their time to appreciate the diverse works of art.
During the art class, the students were encouraged to take their time with their paintings.
In the peaceful garden, we took our time enjoying the beautiful scenery.

senses.definition: to need a significant amount of time to be able to happen, be completed, or achieved

examples.x: Learning a new language can take time, patience, and practice.
He takes time to build strong relationships with his colleagues.
Recovery from an injury can take time and physical therapy.

senses.definition: to require a period of time to happen, be completed, or do something

examples.x: I need to take a while to think about this.
The repairs on the house will take a while to finish.
Learning a new skill can take a while, so be patient with yourself.
She is aware that building trust takes a while in any relationship.
It took a while for the chef to prepare the elaborate meal for the guests.

senses.definition: to require a very long time to complete something, often much longer than expected or necessary

examples.x: Sometimes, finding a parking space in the city takes ages.
She took ages to finish her intricate artwork.
Waiting for the bus on a cold day feels like it takes ages.

senses.definition: to consume or use illegal substances, such as heroin or cocaine, by the use of mouth, injection, or inhalation

examples.x: It is important to be cautious and responsible when taking drugs, as they can have serious side effects and impacts on one's health and well-being.
Some people choose to take drugs to escape from their problems or to enhance their experiences.
The person took drugs to commit suicide.
The addict took drugs to escape from reality.
The teenager took drugs to fit in with his friends.

senses.definition: to assume control or responsibility for something or someone

examples.x: In times of crisis, she is always ready to take charge and make important decisions.
He took charge of the project, leading the team to successful completion.
They will take charge of the event's organization, ensuring it runs smoothly.
As a manager, he knows when to take charge and guide his team toward goals.
During emergencies, it's crucial for someone to take charge and coordinate efforts.

senses.definition: to accept or receive an expression of dissatisfaction or criticism from a customer, client, etc. regarding a product, service, or experience

examples.x: The hotel's front desk staff is available to take a complaint from any guest with concerns about their stay.

senses.definition: to not treat a situation or problem with the seriousness or importance it deserves

examples.x: She didn't take lightly the responsibility of leading the project.
It's important not to take lightly the potential risks of starting a new business.
He realized too late that he had taken lightly the warning about the severe weather.
The coach made it clear that he would not take lightly any breach of the team's code of conduct.
You shouldn't take lightly the advice given by experienced professionals.
